inspectionMethod: Visual with feeler gauge; measure radial clearance at hinge
notes: Inboard hinge experiences highest bending loads during roll maneuvers.

inspectionMethod: Visual with feeler gauge at mid-hinge location
notes: Mid-span hinge takes distributed loading from aileron chord.

inspectionMethod: Visual with feeler gauge at outboard hinge
notes: Outboard hinge is farthest from fuselage attach; may see highest dynamic stress.

displayName: Aileron L Pushrod Inboard Rod-End Bearing Slop
inspectionMethod: Manual deflection check with feel; measure slop with dial indicator under load
notes: Inboard rod-end experiences direct cable pull and fuselage-mounted reaction forces.

displayName: Aileron L Pushrod Outboard Rod-End Bearing Slop
inspectionMethod: Manual deflection check with feel; measure slop at aileron attachment
notes: Outboard rod-end transmits control force directly to aileron horn.

displayName: Aileron L Balance Weight Attachment Rivet Looseness
anpTerm: Rivet Pull-Through or Fastener Preload Loss
inspectionMethod: Visual inspection and tap test for rivet reseating; check balance weight position
notes: Loose balance weight shifts CG and reduces aerodynamic balance, increasing flutter susceptibility.

displayName: Aileron L Trailing Edge Skin Oil-Canning Depth
inspectionMethod: Visual from below during flight or on jacks; feel surface with hand
notes: Oil-canning indicates skin fatigue and stiffness loss; progressive buckling increases flutter risk.

displayName: Aileron L Control Horn Rivet Looseness Count
anpTerm: Fastener Preload Loss or Pull-Through Count
inspectionMethod: Visual tap test of control horn; check for recessed rivet heads or gaps
notes: Loose rivets at control horn attachment reduce structural stiffness and allow freeplay.

inspectionMethod: Measure stop engagement depth with depth gauge at full up and full down
notes: Worn stop allows aileron to move beyond design limit, risking structural damage.

displayName: Aileron L Skin Chordwise Crack Count
inspectionMethod: Dye penetrant or eddy current scan of aileron skin spanwise
notes: Chordwise cracks indicate fatigue progression and allow flutter propagation.

inspectionMethod: Visual with feeler gauge; measure radial clearance at hinge
notes: Right inboard hinge wears symmetrically with left side.

inspectionMethod: Visual with feeler gauge at mid-hinge location
notes: Right mid-span hinge wears symmetrically with left.

inspectionMethod: Visual with feeler gauge at outboard hinge
notes: Right outboard hinge mirrors left wear pattern.

displayName: Aileron R Pushrod Inboard Rod-End Bearing Slop
inspectionMethod: Manual deflection check with feel; measure slop with dial indicator under load
notes: Right inboard rod-end wears in tandem with left.

displayName: Aileron R Pushrod Outboard Rod-End Bearing Slop
inspectionMethod: Manual deflection check with feel; measure slop at aileron attachment
notes: Right outboard rod-end wear mirrors left side.

displayName: Aileron R Balance Weight Attachment Rivet Looseness
anpTerm: Rivet Pull-Through or Fastener Preload Loss
inspectionMethod: Visual inspection and tap test for rivet reseating; check balance weight position
notes: Right balance weight attachment wear mirrors left.

displayName: Aileron R Trailing Edge Skin Oil-Canning Depth
inspectionMethod: Visual from below during flight or on jacks; feel surface with hand
notes: Right trailing edge oil-canning mirrors left side.

displayName: Aileron R Control Horn Rivet Looseness Count
anpTerm: Fastener Preload Loss or Pull-Through Count
inspectionMethod: Visual tap test of control horn; check for recessed rivet heads or gaps
notes: Right control horn rivet wear mirrors left side.

inspectionMethod: Measure stop engagement depth with depth gauge at full up and full down
notes: Right aileron stop wear mirrors left side.

displayName: Aileron R Skin Chordwise Crack Count
inspectionMethod: Dye penetrant or eddy current scan of aileron skin spanwise
notes: Right skin cracks mirror left side fatigue.

inspectionMethod: Tensiometer on aileron cable at mid-span; measure under no-load condition
notes: Low cable tension increases control freeplay and trim load.

anpTerm: Pulley Groove Depth or Flat-Spot Development
inspectionMethod: Visual inspection under fuselage; measure groove depth with depth gauge
notes: Pulley wear increases cable friction and reduces tension feedback.

anpTerm: Pulley Bearing Radial Clearance or Rotational Drag
inspectionMethod: Spin pulley by hand; measure with dial indicator under light load
notes: Seized or tight pulley bearing increases cable friction and control resistance.

anpTerm: Fairlead Wear or Cable Jacket Abrasion Depth
inspectionMethod: Visual inspection of cable jacket near fairleads; check for white braiding exposed
notes: Chafed fairleads can lead to cable failure and loss of aileron control.

anpTerm: Chain Link Elongation or Pitch Increase
inspectionMethod: Measure chain length under no-load condition using reference marks or caliper
notes: Chain stretch reduces control precision and increases freeplay.

inspectionMethod: Visual with feeler gauge; measure radial clearance at upper hinge
notes: Upper elevator hinge takes bending loads from pitch control and vertical gusts.

inspectionMethod: Visual with feeler gauge at mid-span hinge
notes: Mid-hinge distributes bending loads from control and gust loads.

inspectionMethod: Visual with feeler gauge at lower hinge
notes: Lower hinge takes significant moment arm from control forces.

displayName: Elevator L Push-Pull Tube Rod-End Bearing Slop
inspectionMethod: Manual deflection check with feel; measure slop with dial indicator
notes: Tube rod-end transmits direct control force from yoke.

inspectionMethod: Measure stop depth at full up and full down with depth gauge
notes: Worn elevator stop allows over-deflection and structural stress.

displayName: Elevator L Mass Balance Lead Corrosion Depth
anpTerm: Lead Oxide Crust Thickness or Lead Loss by Weight
inspectionMethod: Visual inspection of lead balance weight; weigh if accessible; check for white oxide crust
notes: Lead corrosion shifts CG aft, reducing aerodynamic balance and increasing flutter risk.

displayName: Elevator L Skin Trailing Edge Condition
anpTerm: Skin Separation, Oil-Canning, or Crack Count
inspectionMethod: Visual inspection of trailing edge join; feel for gaps or separation with feeler gauge
notes: Trailing edge separation or cracks increase flutter susceptibility.

inspectionMethod: Visual with feeler gauge; measure radial clearance at upper hinge
notes: Right upper hinge wears symmetrically with left.

inspectionMethod: Visual with feeler gauge at mid-span hinge
notes: Right mid-hinge mirrors left wear pattern.

inspectionMethod: Visual with feeler gauge at lower hinge
notes: Right lower hinge wears with left side.

displayName: Elevator R Push-Pull Tube Rod-End Bearing Slop
inspectionMethod: Manual deflection check with feel; measure slop with dial indicator
notes: Right tube rod-end wears symmetrically with left.

inspectionMethod: Measure stop depth at full up and full down with depth gauge
notes: Right elevator stop wears with left side.

displayName: Elevator R Mass Balance Lead Corrosion Depth
anpTerm: Lead Oxide Crust Thickness or Lead Loss by Weight
inspectionMethod: Visual inspection of lead balance weight; weigh if accessible; check for white oxide crust
notes: Right balance lead corrosion mirrors left side.

displayName: Elevator R Skin Trailing Edge Condition
anpTerm: Skin Separation, Oil-Canning, or Crack Count
inspectionMethod: Visual inspection of trailing edge join; feel for gaps or separation with feeler gauge
notes: Right trailing edge wears with left side.

inspectionMethod: Measure spring force with spring scale applied to elevator neutral position
notes: Low down-spring tension requires more trim input to hold pitch attitude.

inspectionMethod: Tensiometer on elevator cable; measure under no-load condition
notes: Low cable tension increases pitch control freeplay.

anpTerm: Hinge Bushing Clearance or Rivet Looseness
inspectionMethod: Visual with feeler gauge at trim tab hinge; check rivet preload with tap test
notes: Slack trim tab hinge increases flutter risk and trim effectiveness variation.

displayName: Trim Tab Actuator Screw Thread Wear (Axial Slop)
inspectionMethod: Measure screw end-play by hand under no-load condition; use dial indicator if needed
notes: Screw wear reduces trim actuator precision and increases runaway risk.

anpTerm: Cable Elongation Percentage or Absolute Length Increase
inspectionMethod: Measure total trim cable length under reference load; compare to baseline or spec
notes: Cable stretch reduces trim range and control authority.

displayName: Trim Drum Cable Wrap Wear (Groove Depth)
anpTerm: Drum Groove Deepening or Cable Jacket Wear
inspectionMethod: Visual inspection of trim drum; measure groove depth with depth gauge
notes: Worn drum groove allows cable slip and can cause uncontrolled trim movement.

anpTerm: Detent Spring Load or Detent Ball Wear
inspectionMethod: Measure detent friction torque with torque wrench applied to trim wheel under load
notes: Loss of detent friction allows trim wheel to creep or rotate freely under acceleration.

inspectionMethod: Visual with feeler gauge at top hinge; measure radial clearance
notes: Top rudder hinge takes vertical bending from fin aerodynamic load.

inspectionMethod: Visual with feeler gauge at mid-span hinge
notes: Mid-hinge distributes bending load from yaw control.

inspectionMethod: Visual with feeler gauge at lower hinge
notes: Bottom hinge supports lower rudder span and tail boom reaction moment.

inspectionMethod: Tensiometer on left rudder cable at mid-span; measure under no-load condition
notes: Low left cable tension reduces directional control authority.

inspectionMethod: Tensiometer on right rudder cable at mid-span; measure under no-load condition
notes: Low right cable tension reduces directional control authority.

inspectionMethod: Visual with feeler gauge at left pedal pivot; check radial slop
notes: Loose pedal pivot reduces control precision and can cause autopilot hunting.

inspectionMethod: Visual with feeler gauge at right pedal pivot; check radial slop
notes: Loose right pedal pivot reduces directional control feel.

displayName: Rudder Bungee Spring Permanent Set (Force Loss)
anpTerm: Spring Centering Force or Return-to-Center Load
inspectionMethod: Measure bungee return force with fish scale applied to pedal after full deflection
notes: Weakened bungee spring causes rudder to drift off center, inducing autopilot corrections.

inspectionMethod: Measure stop depth at full left and full right deflection with depth gauge
notes: Worn stops allow rudder over-deflection and structural overstress.

displayName: Rudder Tail Tie-Down Ring Corrosion Depth
anpTerm: Corrosion Loss Thickness or Ring Diameter Loss
inspectionMethod: Visual inspection and measurement with calipers; check for white oxide or pitting
notes: Corroded tie-down ring weakens tail mooring during ground operations.

anpTerm: Roller Diameter Loss or Flat-Spot Depth
inspectionMethod: Visual inspection of roller; measure with micrometer at multiple points on roller circumference
notes: Worn roller 1 (upper track attachment) increases track friction and flap extension torque.

anpTerm: Roller Diameter Loss or Flat-Spot Depth
inspectionMethod: Visual inspection of roller; measure with micrometer at mid-track position
notes: Worn roller 2 (mid-track) affects mid-stroke flap extension force.

anpTerm: Roller Diameter Loss or Flat-Spot Depth
inspectionMethod: Visual inspection of roller; measure with micrometer at lower track position
notes: Worn roller 3 (lower track attachment) increases retraction torque.

anpTerm: Roller Diameter Loss or Flat-Spot Depth
inspectionMethod: Visual inspection of roller; measure with micrometer at trailing edge attachment
notes: Worn roller 4 at trailing edge affects final flap deployment positions.

inspectionMethod: Visual with feeler gauge; measure radial clearance at flap hinge
notes: Worn hinge bushing allows flap freeplay and increases flutter susceptibility.

displayName: Flap L Pushrod Rod-End Bearing Slop
inspectionMethod: Manual deflection check with feel; measure slop with dial indicator
notes: Pushrod slop increases flap asymmetry and control lag.

anpTerm: Crack Count or Skin Separation Area
inspectionMethod: Visual inspection of flap upper and lower surfaces; dye penetrant if cracks suspected
notes: Flap skin cracks allow stress concentration and progressive failure.

displayName: Flap L Actuator Attachment Point Rivet Looseness
anpTerm: Rivet Pull-Through or Fastener Preload Loss
inspectionMethod: Visual tap test of actuator attach point; check for recessed rivets or gaps
notes: Loose attachment rivets allow flap lateral movement and asymmetric deployment.

inspectionMethod: Measure stop depth at full retract and full extend positions
notes: Worn flap stop allows over-extension and structural overstress.

anpTerm: Roller Diameter Loss or Flat-Spot Depth
inspectionMethod: Visual inspection of roller; measure with micrometer at multiple points on roller circumference
notes: Right flap roller 1 wear mirrors left side.

anpTerm: Roller Diameter Loss or Flat-Spot Depth
inspectionMethod: Visual inspection of roller; measure with micrometer at mid-track position
notes: Right flap roller 2 wear mirrors left.

anpTerm: Roller Diameter Loss or Flat-Spot Depth
inspectionMethod: Visual inspection of roller; measure with micrometer at lower track position
notes: Right flap roller 3 wear mirrors left.

anpTerm: Roller Diameter Loss or Flat-Spot Depth
inspectionMethod: Visual inspection of roller; measure with micrometer at trailing edge attachment
notes: Right flap roller 4 wear mirrors left.

inspectionMethod: Visual with feeler gauge; measure radial clearance at flap hinge
notes: Right flap hinge wear mirrors left.

displayName: Flap R Pushrod Rod-End Bearing Slop
inspectionMethod: Manual deflection check with feel; measure slop with dial indicator
notes: Right pushrod slop mirrors left side.

anpTerm: Crack Count or Skin Separation Area
inspectionMethod: Visual inspection of flap upper and lower surfaces; dye penetrant if cracks suspected
notes: Right flap skin cracks mirror left side.

displayName: Flap R Actuator Attachment Point Rivet Looseness
anpTerm: Rivet Pull-Through or Fastener Preload Loss
inspectionMethod: Visual tap test of actuator attach point; check for recessed rivets or gaps
notes: Right actuator attachment rivets mirror left side.

inspectionMethod: Measure stop depth at full retract and full extend positions
notes: Right flap stop wear mirrors left.

anpTerm: Brush Length or Remaining Material Percentage
inspectionMethod: Visual inspection of motor commutator and brush cartridge; measure brush height with vernier
notes: Short brushes reduce motor efficiency and increase electrical arcing risk.

displayName: Flap Actuator Jackscrew Thread Wear (Axial Slop)
anpTerm: Thread Pitch Loss or Lead Error
inspectionMethod: Measure screw axial play by hand under no-load condition; use dial indicator if needed
notes: Jackscrew wear allows asymmetric flap deployment and actuator malfunction.

displayName: Flap Actuator Limit Switch Electrical Contact Wear
anpTerm: Switch Contact Resistance or Engagement Margin Loss
inspectionMethod: Measure switch contact resistance with ohmmeter at limit positions; check for pitting with magnification
notes: Worn limit switch contacts may fail to stop motor at full extension or retraction.

displayName: Flap Position Sender Potentiometer Wear (Resistance Variation)
anpTerm: Output Linearity Error or Noise Amplitude
inspectionMethod: Measure potentiometer resistance at known positions; plot linearity curve for drift or noise
notes: Worn potentiometer can produce false flap position signals to the autopilot or flight display.

displayName: Flap Motor Circuit Breaker Spring Fatigue (Trip Threshold Shift)
anpTerm: Trip Current Hysteresis or Preload Loss Percentage
inspectionMethod: Compare breaker trip current to new baseline with electronic breaker tester or calibrated load source
notes: Fatigued breaker may trip prematurely on normal current draw or fail to protect on overload.
